question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?>)] Can an unprivileged external user inject a crafted StreamFile with a null getType() return value into the verified() dispatch path, causing a NullPointerException in the switch statement inside BatchStreamFileNotifier.verified() that crashes the processing thread and halts >= 30% of stream file ingestion?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?>)] Can an unprivileged external user supply a StreamFile whose getType() returns an unsupported enum value (not BALANCE or RECORD), triggering the IllegalArgumentException thrown in BatchStreamFileNotifier.verified() and causing the caller thread to abort, stalling >= 30% of network processing nodes?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: notify(StreamFile<?>)] Can an unprivileged external user flood the ArrayBlockingQueue in StreamFileSubscriber.notify() by continuously submitting verified stream files faster than the parser consumes them, causing queue.put() to block indefinitely and stalling the downloader thread that calls verified(), effectively shutting down >= 30% of processing nodes?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: notify(StreamFile<?>)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the @SneakyThrows annotation on StreamFileSubscriber.notify() to cause a hidden InterruptedException from queue.put() to propagate as an unchecked exception to the caller, disrupting the downloader thread's control flow and halting processing for >= 30% of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: run()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a scenario where the StreamFileSubscriber.run() loop exits prematurely by causing an InterruptedException that sets Thread.currentThread().interrupt() and returns, permanently stopping the subscriber thread and halting >= 30% of stream file processing?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: handle(StreamFile<?>)] Can an unprivileged external user craft a StreamFile that causes streamFileParser.parse() to throw an uncaught RuntimeException inside handle(), which is caught and logged but leaves the buffer, files, and items counters in a dirty state (reset() is never called), causing all subsequent batch flushes to be corrupted and stalling >= 30% of processing nodes?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: flush(StreamFile<?>)] Can an unprivileged external user inject a StreamFile with a far-future consensusEnd timestamp so that the lag calculation DomainUtils.now() - streamFile.getConsensusEnd() produces a large negative value, permanently preventing the window-based flush condition from triggering and causing the buffer to grow unbounded until the queue blocks, stalling >= 30% of processing nodes?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: flush(StreamFile<?>)] Can an unprivileged external user craft a StreamFile with getCount() returning Long.MAX_VALUE, causing items.addAndGet(streamFile.getCount()) to overflow the AtomicLong in flush(), producing incorrect flush decisions that either skip necessary flushes or trigger excessive flushes, degrading processing throughput across >= 30% of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?>)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the CompositeStreamFileNotifier.verified() loop's lack of exception handling so that if the first notifier in the list throws a RuntimeException, all subsequent notifiers (including BatchStreamFileNotifier) are skipped, causing partial or complete loss of stream file processing for >= 30% of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?>)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a StreamFile with a null getName() or null getHash() value that propagates through CompositeStreamFileNotifier.verified() into downstream notifiers, causing NullPointerExceptions that abort processing pipelines and shut down >= 30% of network processing nodes?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: run()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the StreamFileSubscriber.run() loop to spin indefinitely on queue.poll() with a zero or near-zero frequency value derived from streamFileParser.getProperties().getFrequency().toMillis(), consuming 100% CPU on the subscriber thread and starving other processing threads, effectively shutting down >= 30% of processing capacity?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: handle(StreamFile<?>)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a sequence of StreamFiles where each parse() call throws an exception, causing the buffer ArrayList to grow without bound (since reset() is never called on exception), eventually causing an OutOfMemoryError that crashes the JVM and shuts down >= 30% of processing nodes?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: flush(StreamFile<?>)] Can an unprivileged external user craft a StreamFile with getCount() returning a negative value, causing items.addAndGet() to decrement the AtomicLong counter below the maxItems threshold indefinitely, preventing the item-count-based flush from ever triggering and causing the buffer to grow until memory exhaustion halts >= 30% of processing nodes?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?>)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that BatchStreamFileNotifier.verified() dispatches to recordStreamFileSubscriber for RECORD type files and simultaneously flood both the BALANCE and RECORD queues to capacity, causing both queue.put() calls to block and deadlocking the two fixed-thread-pool threads, shutting down >= 30% of processing nodes?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: close()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a scenario where BatchStreamFileNotifier.close() is called while queue.put() is blocking in notify(), causing the ExecutorService to shut down but leaving the blocked caller thread permanently suspended, halting >= 30% of stream file processing?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: exceedsInterval()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a clock skew or system time manipulation to make Duration.between(lastFlush.get(), Instant.now()) always return a negative or zero duration, preventing exceedsInterval() from ever returning true and causing the buffer to never flush via the interval path, stalling >= 30% of processing nodes?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: handle(StreamFile<?>)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a StreamFile where streamFile.getCount() returns 0 and the consensusEnd is far in the future, causing flush() to always return false (no maxFiles, no maxItems, no window, no interval), so the buffer accumulates indefinitely and the queue eventually blocks, stalling >= 30% of processing nodes?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: run()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the StreamFileSubscriber.run() loop to catch a non-InterruptedException exception from handle() on every iteration (e.g., by injecting a StreamFile that always causes parse() to throw), causing the loop to spin in a tight error-log cycle that saturates I/O and CPU, degrading >= 30% of processing nodes?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: flush(StreamFile<?>)] Can an unprivileged external user craft a StreamFile with getCount() returning Integer.MAX_VALUE repeatedly, causing items.addAndGet() to overflow to a negative value after two files, resetting the effective item count and preventing maxItems-based flushes from triggering, causing the buffer to grow and eventually block the queue, stalling >= 30% of processing nodes?
